title: ScarFireEvent
description: Log entry for a functional scar hook fire. Append to fires.jsonl (one JSON object per line). See docs/metrics.md for how these fields power the 9 Phase 2 metrics.

session_id.description: Claude Code session identifier. Use the first 8 chars of a hash of the session start timestamp + working directory.

event_id.description: UUID v4, unique per fire event.

scar_id.description: Scar identifier. Examples: scar_001, scar_004, scar_011.

hook_id.description: Script name without .py extension. Example: hook_scar_001_docx

hook_version.description: Semantic version of the hook script at fire time. Example: 1.2.0. Increment when the filter logic changes.

model.description: Active LLM model ID. Example: claude-sonnet-4-6

project_id.description: Identifier for the working directory / project. Use a short hash or slug. Sanitize — no full paths.

event_type.description: Claude Code hook event type that triggered this fire.

tool_name.description: Tool name that triggered the PreToolUse/PostToolUse event. Null for SessionStart and UserPromptSubmit.

trigger_match.description: Fragment of the input that matched the hook filter. Keep short (<200 chars). Used for filter refinement.

latency_ms.description: Hook execution time in milliseconds. Measures overhead added to each tool call.

tokens_added.description: Total tokens injected by this hook fire (additionalContext + systemMessage combined).

severity.description: Severity level of this scar at fire time. Most scars start at 'warn'. See escalation-policy.md.

outcome.description: Result of the hook fire. 'prevented': error did not occur. 'leaked': error occurred despite fire. 'false_positive': hook fired but error was not imminent. 'unknown': not yet reviewed. Set by operator after reviewing the session.

criticidad.description: Criticality level of the scar as defined in scar_NNN.md.

payload_hash.description: SHA-256 of the tool_input that triggered the hook. Enables reproducibility without logging sensitive content. Format: sha256:<hex>.

reviewed_by_human.description: True if the operator has reviewed this entry and set outcome/false_positive manually. False means 'outcome' is still 'unknown'.

notes.description: Optional operator notes. Useful for capturing why a fire was a false positive or what the error looked like.
